titlestmt.author: Compiled by Patricia Prunty (1981) and Adrienne Scholtz (1990)

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 842122.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to local Music Library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 00:25

did.unittitle: The Hershy Kay Papers
did.abstract: Music, programs, clippings, photographs, and other materials by and about the American composer, arranger, and orchestrator Hershy Kay (1919-1981)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The Papers are open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Hershy Kay Papers were established in the Music Library of Yale University by Kay in 1979. His wife, Maria Kay, made a second gift in 1982.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Hershy Kay Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 28, The Hershy Kay Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In 8 series as follows: I. Music by Kay. II. Music Arranged by Kay. III. Music by Others. IV. Programs. V. Clippings. VI. Photographs. VII. Writings by Others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Hershy Kay Papers contain sketches, manuscript scores, and printed editions of Kay's musical compositions and arrangements, including ballets, orchestral works, and songs. The Papers also hold music by other composers, books, photographs, and posters.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hershy Kay was born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, on November 17, 1919. He studied cello and orchestration at the Curtis Institute of Music. Although he produced some original compositions, Kay became best known as an arranger and orchestrator for the New York City Ballet and a wide range of Broadway musicals (including several by Leonard Bernstein). Hershy Kay died in Danbury, Connecticut on December 2, 1981.

odd.head: Index to Hershy Kay's Music and Arrangements

row.entry:
Concert
See: Chopin, Frederic. [Piano music. Selections; arr.]

Le Corsair
See: Delibes, Léo. [Valse, ou Pas de fleurs; arr.]

Golden Age
See: Pitot, Genevieve. [Golden age; arr.]

Grand Tour
See: Coward, Noel. [Grand tour; arr.]

Meadow lark
See: Haydn, Joseph. [Chamber music. Selections; arr.]

Slaughter on Tenth Avenue
See: Rodgers, Richard. [On Your Toes. Slaughter on Tenth Avenue; arr.]

La Ventana
See: Lumbye, H.C. (Hans Christian). [Ventana; arr.]

Who Cares?
See: Gershwin, George. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Air
See: Adam, Adolph. [Poupée de Nuremberg; arr.]

Don't Doubt Me
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Early Springtime
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Ich Wollt' Meine Lieb Ergoesse
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Moonlight Night
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Siete Canciones Populares Españolas
See: Falla, Manuel de. [Canciones populares españolas; arr.]

Star-spangled Banner
See: Smith, John Stafford. [Star-spangled banner; arr.]

Suite Espagnole
See: Falla, Manuel de. [Canciones populares españolas; arr.]

War Ich Nicht ein Halm
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Where Dancing was Loudest
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

Whether by Day
See: Tchaikovsky, Peter. [Songs. Selections; arr.]

L'Escarpolette
See: Bizet, Georges. [Jeux d'enfants. Selections; arr.]

Funérailles
See: Liszt, Franz. [Harmonies poétiques et religieuses. Funérailles; arr.]

Grand Tarantelle
See: Gottschalk, Louis Moreau. [Grand tarantelle; arr.]
Variations on Joy to the World

Kyrie, Gott Heiliger Geist
See: Bach, J.S. [Kyrie, Gott heiliger Geist, BWV 671; arr.]

did.unittitle: Music by Kay

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The music is arranged alphabetically and uniform titles are used to bring together all of the manifestations of a work with different titles.
Boxes and folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes holograph, manuscript, and published music by Hershy Kay.

did.unittitle: [Adagio] Adagio / Vay Burne.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Vay Burne is probably a pseudonym for Kay

did.unittitle: [Cakewalk] Cakewalk / from the music of Louis Moreau Gottschalk ; Adapted & Orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; Choreography [by] Ruthanna Boris.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript (photocopy) with pen and pencil annotations

Contents: I. Opening Grand Walkaround. IIA. Wallflower Waltz. IIB. Sleight of Feet. IIC. Perpendicular Points. III. Freebee. IVA. Throw-away. IVB. Magic Act Entrance of Magicians. IVC. The Wild Pony. IVD. Pas de Deux. IVE. Exit. V. Finale-Gala Cakewalk.
Includes emendations and segues pasted in

On t.p.: Music Sources - Bamboula, op. 2, La Gallina, op. 53, The Banjo, op. 15, Danse des sylphes, op. 86 [crossed out], Pasquinade, op. 59, Marche des Giberos, 1859 [crossed out], Maiden's blush, Orfa, The water sprite, Dandy black brigade, James Bland [crossed out], Won't you buy my pretty flowers, G.W. Persley, Adolphus Morning Glory, D. Braham, Josephus Orange Blossom, Anon.
Durations of each number and total listed in pencil on front fly-leaf
Program and clippings pasted to verso of front and back covers

did.unittitle: [Cakewalk] Cakewalk

odd.head: General
odd.p: Adapted from music by Louis Moreau Gottschalk
Incomplete: score includes pp. 37-38, 119, 130, 133, 140-142, 159-176, 174-179, 186-192; and parts (vl. I-II, vla., and vlc.) are from measure 19 to end, with 10 partial sheets laid in

did.unittitle: [Cakewalk; arr.] Cakewalk / from the music of Louis Moreau Gottschalk ; Adapted & Orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; choreography [by] Ruthanna Boris.
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript piano score (47 p.), bound ; 33 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript (photocopy) with pencil additions [III. Freebee, is a photocopy of printed music]

Contents: I. Opening-Grand walkaround. IIA. Wallflower waltz. IIB. Sleight of feet. IIC. Perpendicular points. III. Freebee. IVA. Throw-away. IVB. Magic act-entrance of magicians. IVC. The wild pony. IVD. Pas de deux. IVE. Rideout. V. Finale-Grand cakewalk

On t.p.: Music Sources, Bamboula, op. 2, La Gallina, op. 53, The banjo, op. 15, Danse des sylphes, op. 86, Pasquinade, op. 59, Marche de Giberos, 1859, Maiden's blush, Orfa, The water sprite, Won't you buy my pretty flowers?, Adolphus Morning Glory, Josephus Orange Blossom

did.unittitle: [Cakewalk. Finale] V. Finale - Grand cakewalk

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sticker on cover indicates Boosey & Hawkes

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in ink, in part positive and negative photocopies

Includes: 7 string parts (manuscript in ink, transparencies); 20 parts (negative photocopies) with note laid in "to be copied...Fix," etc. ; and 21 parts (woodwind, brass, harp, and piano/celeste - reverse photocopies)

did.unittitle: [Cakewalk. Suite; arr.] Cakewalk suite / Hershy Kay ; Arranged by William Chaloner. -- [S.l.] : Boosey & Hawkes, c1969.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For band; originally adapted for orchestra as a ballet in one act from music by Louis Moreau Gottschalk
Contents: 1. Grand walkaround. 2. Wallflower waltz. 3. Sleight of feet. 4. Entrance of the magicians. 5. Gala cakewalk

did.unittitle: [Clowns] The Clowns : A Ballet / Music [by] Hershy Kay ; Choreography [by] Gerald Arpino.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note on sheet with instrumentation: The score was commissioned by Robert Joffrey Ballet Company
Bound photocopy with pencil additions laid in with reviews of premiere pasted to back cover, and program originally laid in now with PROGRAMS

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes sheet with tone row illustrations, and sheet with pencil sketch "Molto Adagio" laid in

did.unittitle: [Clowns] The clowns / By Hershy Kay.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Clipping with a photograph of Clowns from the New York Times Magazine, 1968 May 12, pasted on an old cover laid in with string parts

did.unittitle: [Clowns. Adagio] Adagio : (Pas de Deux)
[Clowns; arr.] The clowns / Hershy Kay.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arranged for solo violin and instrumental ensemble

did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (32 p. in various pagings) ; 35 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript (photocopy) with pencil and red ink annotations
Arranged for solo violin and instrumental ensemble
First page of Part I-D marked "Piu mosso 1/4/68"

did.unittitle: [Collection of Japanese songs] [Collection of Japanese Songs] / arr. [by] Hershy Kay.
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score ([38] sheets in various pagings) ; 36-43 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: #36 [#36 written in pencil]. Kiso-Bushi (Dance-Song of the Mountaineers). Hakone Hachiri. Bon Odori (All Souls Day Dance-Song). Machiboke. Yuyake to Kari (Evening Song and Wild Geese). Kuruka kuruka. Tsuki (The moon). Gonbe ga tane Maku. Lullaby. Japanese Folk Dance. Hano no Uta. Sakura Sakura.
Title from previous cataloging - possibly from original discarded envelope?

did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (42 p. in various pagings) ; 34-46 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in pencil and ink (in part, photocopies with annotations)

Contents: Gonbe ga tane Maku. #36. Sakura Sakura. Hano no Uta. Folk dance. Lullaby. Tsuki (The Moon). Kuruka kuruka. (#29). Yuyake to Kari (Evening Song and Wild Geese). Machiboke. Bon Odori (All Souls Day Dance Song). Hakone Hachiri (#21). Kiso-Bushi (Dance-Song of Kiso Mountaineers)
Originally contained in large envelope marked "from Mr. Leo Mueller For Mr. Hershy Kay"

did.unittitle: [Cortège burlesque] Cortège burlesque : music "Cortège Burlesque" and "Souvenirs de Munich" / by Emmanuel Chabrier ; Orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; Choreography by Eliot Feld.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Bound copy (66 p. ; 36 cm.) with additions in red ink, laid in

odd.p: Discarded envelope marked B & H [i.e. Boosey & Hawkes]

odd.p: Dedicated to George Szell and the Cleveland Orchestra

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dedicated to the Woodbury Concert Band
Reviews originally laid in now with CLIPPINGS

did.unittitle: [Inconnue] L'Inconnue / [by] Hershy Kay.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Originally in envelope labeled: B & H [i.e. Boosey & Hawkes]
Extra sheets [112-115] laid in, had note attached "Do not print"
Photocopy (bound ; 43 cm.) laid in with reviews and programs for the American Ballet Theatre premiere [1965] pasted to the front and back cover versos, See: PROGRAMS, CLIPPINGS

did.unittitle: [Maske] Maske : a ballet / Music adapted and orchestrated by Hershy Kay from 15th and 16th century lute music ; choreography [by] Eliot Feld.

odd.head: General
odd.p: On t.p.: Commissioned by The Royal Danish Ballet Copenhagen 25 April 1972

Contents: Overture. Mignarda / John Dowland. 1. Fantasia / Dlugoraj. 2. Fantasia / Mudarra. 3. Sir John Smith's Almain / John Dowland. 4. Lord D'Lisle's Galliard / John Dowland. 5. Batell Galliard / John Dowland. 6. Fantasia (Fancy) / John Dowland. 7. Finale / Dlugoraj. 8. Fantasia / John Dowland. 9. Finale / Dlugoraj. 10. Ich klag den Tag / Hans Newsidler. 11. Fantasia / Dlugoraj

did.unittitle: [Maske. Selections; arr.] Maske : a ballet
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript piano score (15 p. in various pagings) ; 28-38 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Title from photocopy of title page laid in
Music adapted and orchestrated by Hershy Kay from 15th and 16th century lute music; choreography Eliot Feld; commissioned by The Royal Danish Ballet Copenhagen 25 April 1972

Contents [in part, published music for lute or guitar]: #5. Batell / by John Douland, Batchelar of Musicke (photocopy of published music with annotations, marked pp. 10-12). #4. Lord D'Lisle's Galliard (photocopy of manuscript with pencil additions, 2 p.). #3. Sir John Smith his Almaine / J. Dowland (photocopy of published music with pencil annotations ([2] p.). #2. Fantasia X / Mudara [sic] (photocopy of published music with annotations, 3 p.). #1. Fantasia / Dlugoraj (photocopy of published music with annotations, [1] p.).

Sheet labeled "New order' with list, in pencil, of overture (A) and 11 numbers. #6. Fancye / John Dowland (manuscript in pencil, 4 p.). A. Mignarda / John Dowland (manuscript, [1] p. with sketch on back). Photocopy of t.p. from full score. Photocopy of sheet with order of numbers and orchestration from full score. T.p. information in pencil on stationery from the Hotel Opera, Copenhagen. #6. Fancye / John Dowland (photocopy of manuscript with pencil annotations, 4 p.). #7/#9. Finale / Dlugoraj (photocopy of published music with pencil annotations, [2] p.). #8. Fantasie 6 / Composed by the most famous Gregorio Huwet of Antwerpe...(photocopy of published music with pencil annotations, 3 p.). #7/#9. Finale / Dlugoraj (photocopy of published music with annotations, [1] sheet). #11. Fantasia / Dlugoraj (photocopy of published music with annotations, [1] sheet). A. Mignarda / John Dowland (photocopy of manuscript, [1] sheet). A. Overture (manuscript in pencil, [1] sheet). Galliarde / Dem Konig von Danemark (photocopy of published score, pp. 16-17). Lord D'Lisle's - Galliard (manuscript in pencil, 2 p.). #5. Batell Galliard : Originale pour Luthe / John Dowland ; Realisation pour Guitare Francois Castet. -- Nice : Georges Delrieu, c1964, with annotations, 2 p. photocopy laid in)

did.unittitle: [Mother Goose nursery rhymes] "Mother Goose Nursery Rhymes" / Music composed and arranged by Hershy Kay.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with additions in pencil and red pencil
For voices and spoken voices and piccolo, flute, clarinet, bass clarinet, trumpet, cornet, percussion, and harp

Contents: A. 1. Cackle, cackle, Mother Goose Old Mother Goose. 2. I saw a fish-pond on fire. 3. What are little boys made of? 4. Hector Protector. 5. London Bridge. 6. Peter Piper. 7. Three little kittens. 8. For want of a nail. 9. There was a crooked man. 10. Curley Locks. 11. [lacking]. 12. Little Tommy Tucker. 13. tacet. 14. Jack and Jill went up the hill. 15. Humpty Dumpty. 16. Mr. East gave a feast. 17. Pease porridge hot. 18. Old Mother Hubbard. B. 1. Mary had a little lamb. 2. Monday's child. 3. Tom, Tom, the Piper's son. 4. Pussy cat, pussy cat where have you been? 5. One, two button your shoe. 6. Georgie, Porgie, pudding and pie. 7. The north wind doth blow Poor Thing. 8. Hey diddle, diddle the cat and the fiddle. 9. Little Bo-Peep. 10. There was a man, he went mad. 11. Here am I, little jumping Joan. 12. A frog he would a-wooing go. 13, [lacking]. 14. Little Jack Horner. 15. The old woman must stand at the tub, tub, tub. 16. As I was going to sell my eggs. 17. Twinkle, twinkle, little star. C. 1. [lacking]. 2. Three blind mice. 3. Ride a cock-horse to Banbury Cross. 4. Betty Botter bought some butter. 5. There was a man of double deed. 6. A cat came fiddling out of a barn. 7. A fox jumped up one winter's night. 8. Thirty days hath September. 9. Ba, ba, black sheep. 10. The house that Jack built. 11. There was a little girl, and she had a little curl. 12. Rub-a-dub-dub, three men in a tub. 13. Little boy blue, come blow your horn. D. 1. Old King Cole. 2. I had a little pony, his name was Dapple Gray. 3. Ann Elise, she jumped with surprise. 4. There was a little man. 5. Ding, dong, bell. 6. Three children sliding on the ice. 7. Bumpety , bumpety, bump. 8. Jack Spratt. 9. Little Miss Muffet. 10. Who comes here? 11. Who killed Cock Robin? 12. Old woman. 13. Old woman. 14. There was an old woman. 15. Dill daly Mrs. Bond. 16. Wee Willie Winkel. 17. Go to bed first
Performer's names included in score: Trauble, Baxley, Green, Drake, Karloff

did.unittitle: [Pat-a-pan] Pat-A-Pan : A Fantasy / Hershy Kay. -- N.Y. : Boosey & Hawkes, c1968.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dedicated to George Szell and the Cleveland Orchestra

did.unittitle: [Pat-A-Pan] Pat-a-Pan : a Fantasy / Hershy Kay.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Discarded envelope marked B & H [i.e. Boosey & Hawkes]

did.unittitle: [Pat-a-pan] Pat-a-Pan

odd.p: With: Joy to the world (1 manuscript condensed score (4 p.) ; 32 cm. Holograph in pencil); and Deck (1 manuscript condensed score ([8] p.) ; 32 cm. Holograph in pencil

did.unittitle: [Pat-a-pan; arr.] Pat-a-pan : a fantasy / Hershy Kay ; arranged for Band by Clare Grundman. -- N.Y. : Boosey & Hawkes, c1970.
[Schwangerne Jungfer] Die Schwangerne Jungfer / Stanislaus - Adolphe Hertel ; Edited by Johannes Wanz. -- Fulda Bad Strichmadel : Verlag der Drei Geister, 1492 [sic].
[Stars and stripes] Stars and stripes : ballet suite / Music adapted and orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; after music by John Philip Sousa.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sources listed on t.p.: First movement: "Rifle Regiment" "Corcoran Cadets" "Liberty Bell" Second movement: "Rifle Regiment" "Thunderer" "Gladiator" Third movement: "Washington Post" "Rifle Regiment" Fourth movement: "Picador" "El Capitan" "Liberty Bell" "Manhattan Beach" Fifth movement: "Stars and stripes"
T.p. marked: Commissioned by Ballet Society Inc. for the New York City Ballet...; Boosey & Hawkes
At end: New York City Jan. 13, 1958 7:45 A.M. H.K.
Two copies: copy with blue binding has note on front fly leaf "corrected...3/11/59"; copy with black binding has reviews and premiere program pasted to front cover and fly leaf

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with pencil markings

did.unittitle: [Thief who loved a ghost] The thief who loved a ghost / music: Karl Maria Von Weber; arranged and orchestrated by Hershy Kay; choreography: Herbert Rosso and John Ward.

odd.head: General
odd.p: On t.p.: Music Sources: Ungarische Fantasie for Bassoon op. 35, Four Hand Piece op. 60, Der Freischutz, Euryanthe, Waltz from Sechs Walzer, Piano Sonatas #1 and #3, Clarinet Quintet, Grand Duo for Clarinet and Piano, Abu Harsan, Der Beherrscher der Geister
Program and reviews of premiere pasted to back cover

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in pencil with additions in ink and red pencil
Loosely numbered pages suggest sequence in suite

With: [17] p. xerox scores interspersed - from published scores by Weber, with corrections in pencil, ink, and pasted in; [24] p. negative xerox, Weber's published scores, some pencil notations on reverse; [3] p. incomplete score of "Perpetual motion" from Sonata op. 24 of Weber, (N.Y. : Century Music, 1915) interspersed in manuscript sketches; Weber, Quintette, op. 34 for clarinet and strings (Boston: Lundy-Bettoney Co., Inc.) 35 p.; Weber, Concerto for Bassoon and Orchestra, op. 75 [piano reduction] (Berlin : R.U.W.Lienau, n.d.) 21 p.; Weber, Ungarische Fantasie, op. 35, for bassoon and piano (Heilbrohn : D.F. Schmidt, n.d.), 13 p.; Weber, Quintette, op. 34, parts score, published in conjunction with above

did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score ([272] p. in various pagings) ; 46 cm.

odd.p: Holograph (photocopy) with ink and red pencil additions
Contents: III. Tattoo [title at bottom of page, first page missing]. [Part I]. II. Pas de Duex [i.e., Deux]

odd.head: General

odd.head: General
odd.p: Notes on discarded folder: Mark Wilson / Boosey & Hawkes / Union Jack / (stray vellums)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note laid in [information supplied by Mrs. H. Kay] : Hershy Kay autograph sketches Union Jack, 3d mvt. Revisions requested by Ballanchine. The last writing Hershy did. He died before these changes were incorporated in Union Jack

did.unittitle: Music Arranged by Kay

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The music is arranged alphabetically by composer followed by title. Uniform titles are used to bring together all of the manifestations of a work with different titles.
Boxes and folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes manuscript music by others arranged by Hershy Kay.

did.unittitle: [Poupée de Nuremberg; arr.] Air : (Die Nürnberger Puppe) / [by] Adolph Adam ; orchestration Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arranged for high voice and orchestra

did.unittitle: [Kyrie, Gott heiliger Geist, BWV 671; arr.] Kyrie, Gott heiliger Geist / J.S. Bach ; arr. by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dedication on sheet 2: To Marcel Tabuteau and the Curtis Institute Wind Ensemble

did.unittitle: [Kyrie, Gott heiliger Geist, BWV 671; arr.] Kyrie, Gott heiliger Geist / J.S. Bach ; orchestration for twenty-one wind instruments / by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dedication on p. 1: To Mr. Tabuteau and the Curtis Institute Woodwind Ensemble
Note at the end: First performance - Marcel Tabuteau and the Curtis W.W. Phil.
Three reviews and a program pasted on last page and back cover

did.unittitle: [Jeux d'enfants. Selections; arr.] L'Escarpolette =(The See-Saw) Overture ; Le Volant = Badminton : op. 22 / Georges Bizet ; orchestration Hershy Kay
[Piano music. Selections; arr.] The Concert / [by Chopin] ; [arranged by Hershy Kay]
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (146 p. in various pagings) ; 43 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in ink and pencil, in part copies and published piano part pasted in
Arranged for solo piano and orchestra
This score does not include the unaccompanied solo piano passages

Contents: No. 1. Piano solo orchestra tacet. 1A. Prelude #18. #2. The Struggle (Prelude #20). #3. The Road to Hell (Prelude #16) [2 copies of pp. 15-20 laid in]. #4. Mistake waltz. #5. Preludes (Prelude #7). 6A. Intro to "Minute Waltz" (Prelude #14). #6B. Minute Waltz. #7. Mood of disgust (Prelude #4). #8. Boy's Mazurka. #10. Finale (Ballade op. 47). No. 9. Piano solo orchestra tacet
Table of contents including old and new numbers laid in

did.unittitle: [Piano music. Selections; arr.] Concert / [by] Chopin ; orchestrated by Hershey [sic] Kay
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (142 p. in various pagings), bound ; 43 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arranged for solo piano and orchestra
Score does not include unaccompanied solo piano passages

Contents: #1. Prelude #18 (9 p.). #2. The struggle (Prelude #20) (3 p.). #3. The road to hell (Prelude #16) (20 p.). #4. Mistake waltz (23 p.). No. 4-A. (pp. 15-20). #5. Preludes (Prelude #7) (11 p.). 6A. Intro to "Minute waltz (11 p.). #7. Mood of disgust (Prelude #4) (8 p.). #8. Boy's march (11 p.). No. 9. Piano solo orchestra tacet.#10. Finale (Ballade op. 47) (44 p.). [extra pages laid in at end]

did.unittitle: [Grand tour; arr.] The Grand Tour : a Ballet for the Royal Ballet of London / Music : Noel Coward ; Adapted and orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; Choreography : Joe Layton

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: I. Opening. II. Excersises [sic]. III. IV. Tarantella. VI. Pas de deux. VII. Apotheosis. VI.A. (Alternate ending)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: I. Opening (Mad dogs and Englishmen). II. Exercises (Has anyone seen our ship?) III. (Someday I'll find you). IV. Tarantella (Stately homes of England Mrs. Worthington). V. Half-cast (recording) arr / Percival Mackey. VI. Pas de Deux (If love were all). VII. Apotheosis (The party's over now). At end: Sea-Sick Waltz (Zigeuner), Exotique (I travel alone)

did.unittitle: [Piano music. Selections; arr.] Suite / [by] Claude Debussy ; Orchestrated by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: 1. La Terrasse des Audiences du Clair de Lune [Preludes, book 2]. 2. La Puerta del Vino [Preludes, book 2]. 3. Feuilles Mortes [Preludes, book 2]. 4. Poissons d'or [Images, book 2]

did.unittitle: [Valse, ou Pas de fleurs; arr.] Le Corsair Divertissements / Music [by] Léo Delibes ; orchestrated by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arrangement of a divertissement added to a revival of Adolphe Adam's ballet Le Corsaire

did.unittitle: [Canciones populares españolas; arr.] Siete Canciones Populares Españolas : original key / Composed by Manuel de Falla ; orchestrated by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: 1. El pano moruno. 2. Seguidilla Murciana. 3. Asturiana. 4. Jota. 5. Nana. 6. Canción. 7. Polo
Photocopies of score (bound, with additions) and parts laid in

did.unittitle: [Canciones populares españolas; arr.] Suite Espagnole : for Viola and orchestra / by Manuel de Falla ; Arranged and Orchestrated by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: 1. El pano moruno. 2. Seguidilla Murciana. 3. Asturiana. 4. Jota. 5. Nana. 6. Polo
At end: New York City March 1, 1947

did.unittitle: [Canciones populares españolas; arr.] Suite Espagnole : for viola and piano or orchestra / by Manuel de Falla ; Arranged by Hershy Kay
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (22 sheets) + 1 manuscript part (6 sheets) ; 32 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: 1. El Pano Moruno. 2. Seguidilla Murciana. 3. Asturiana. 4. Jota. 5. Nana. 6. Polo

did.unittitle: [Songs. Selections; arr.] Who Cares? : a Ballet / Music by George Gershwin ; Adapted and Orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; Choreography by George Ballanchine

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: 1. Strike Up the Band (1927). 2. Sweet And Low Down (1925). 3. Somebody Loves Me (1924). 4. Bidin' My Time (1930). 5. 'S Wonderful (1927). 6. That Certain Feeling (1925). 7. Do Do Do (1926). 8. Lady Be Good (1924). 9. The Man I Love (1924). 10. I'll Build A Stairway To Paradise (1922). 11. Embraceable you (1930). 14. My One And Only (1927). 15. Liza (1929). 16. Clap Yo' Hands (1926). 17. I Got Rhythm (1930)
Note on t.p.: Commissioned by New York City Ballet
Reverse transparencies of published songs laid in

did.unittitle: [Selections; arr.] Suite / [by] Orlando Gibbons ; orchestrated by Hershy Kay. -- c1948

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: I. A cornet voluntary. II. Madrigal - Dainty Fine Bird. III. Fantasia

did.unittitle: [Grand tarantelle; arr.] Grand Tarantelle : op. 67 / [by] Louis Moreau Gottschalk ; re-constructed and orchestrated by Hershy Kay
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript score (90 p.), bound + 18 parts ; 32-42 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript (photocopy) with pencil and pen annotations
At end of score: MacDowell Colony Peterborough, New Hampshire July 24, 1957
Program laid in and clippings pasted to verso of front and back covers
Pencil notes on front fly-leaf of score

did.unittitle: [Grand tarantelle; arr.] Grand tarantelle / [by] Gottschalk ; arr. [by] Espadero

odd.head: General
odd.p: Paste-ups from published edition with annotations [by Kay?]
Note: re-constructed from two separate piano copies marked Piano 1 and Piano 2
Title and note from cover of bound copy (33 cm.)

did.unittitle: [Chamber music. Selections; arr.] Meadow lark / Music by Franz Joseph Haydn ; arranged and orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; choreography by Eliot Feld

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sources on t.p.: from Flute Quartet, op. 5; I. #2 (Adagio), II. #2. (Presto), III. #4 (Menuetto), IV. #4 (Fantasia), V. #3 (Presto Assai), VI. #1 (Presto Assai), VII. #4 (Andante moderato); and from String Quartet, op. 74, #1 (Finale-vivace)

did.unittitle: [Harmonies poétiques et religieuses. Funérailles; arr.] Funérailles / [by] Franz Liszt ; orchestrated by Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: On cover: Boosey & Hawkes Rental Library
Liszt's Funerailles for piano with orchestration annotations was laid in, See: MUSIC BY OTHERS

did.unittitle: [Ventana; arr.] La Ventana / orchestration Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Program originally laid in for premiere by the Metropolitan Opera Ballet under the direction of Dame Alicia Markova, reorchestrated by Hershy Kay, staged by Hans Brenaa after the original choreography of Auguste Bournonville, 1966 Mar. 27 [See: PROGRAMS]

did.unittitle: [Golden age; arr.] Golden Age / Music [by] Genevieve Pitot after music by Rossini ; orchestrated [by] Hershy Kay ; choreography [by] Agnes deMille
[On your toes. Slaughter on Tenth Avenue; arr.] Slaughter on Tenth Avenue / Music [by] Richard Rodgers ; orchestrated [by] Hershy Kay ; choreography [by] George Balanchine

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopy (bound ; 44 cm.) laid in [copies of pp. 109-114 are loose at end and revised pp. 109-114 are incorporated into proper place]

did.unittitle: [Star spangled banner; arr.] The Star Spangled Banner / Music by John Stafford Smith ; original harmonization by Thomas Carr; orchestrated by Hershy Kay ; words by Francis Scott Key. -- New York : Walton Music Corporation, c1973

odd.head: General
odd.p: Arranged for chorus (SATB) and orchestra or band with or without choir

Note on p. 2: The choir part contains all the notes played by the instruments with the exception of some octaves, higher and lower. It can be used as a 'short' score by the conductor, as well as by the choir

did.unittitle: [Songs. Selections; arr.] Songs. Selections; arr. / [by] P. Tchaikowsky ; orchestration Hershy Kay

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: Don't Doubt Me [op. 57, no. 3]. Whether by Day [op. 47, no. 6]. Ich Wollt' meine Lieb' ergoesse. Moonlight Night [op. 73, no. 3]. Early Springtime [op. 38, no. 2]. Where Dancing was Loudest [op. 38, no. 3]. War ich nicht ein Halm [op. 47, no. 7]
On bottom of first page of each score: Registered 1948

did.unittitle: Music by Others

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The music is arranged alphabetically by composer followed by title or uniform title. Uniform titles are used to bring together all of the manifestations of a work with different titles.
Boxes and folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes holograph, manuscript, and published music by others.

did.unittitle: The Ballad of Jack the Ripper and other Great Cockney Songs : Vol. 1. -- London : Southern Music Publishing Co. Ltd., c1974

odd.head: General
odd.p: Compiled by James Hume Jr., and Horace Phlange

did.unittitle: [Volksgesänge, piano] Zwei Volksgesänge = Two Folk-Songs : Op. 132 / [by] Ludw. Bonvin. -- Leipzig : Verlag Von Breitkopf & Haertel, n.d.
[Fragmentations] Fragmentations : pour un joueur de harpes. -- Firenze : Aldo Bruzzichelli, c1963
did.physdesc: [4] p. [1 folded sheet] of music ; 44 cm.

did.unittitle: [Siciliano] Siciliano : per dodici voci maschili. -- Firenze : Also Bruzzichelli, c1960
The C.C.F. Drum & Bugle March Book : Revised 1950 Edition Approved by the Joint Cadet Executive Combined Cadet Force / Compiled and Edited by W. R. Patrick. -- London : Boosey & Hawkes, c1951
Cancioneiro de Musicas Populares : para canto e piano / por Cesar A. Das Neves. -- Porto : Typographia Occidental, 1893-1894
Cantos Españoles : Colección de aires nacionales y populares formada e ilustrada con notas esplicativas y biográficas / por Dn. Eduardo Ocón. -- Málaga : s.n., 1874
Cantos populares españoles : para coros mixtos y piano. -- Madrid : Antonio Matamala, c1924
[Concertos, piano, orchestra] Piano concerto / Elliott Carter. -- New York : Associated Music Publishers, Inc., c1967
[Concertos, piano, orchestra; arr.] Piano concerto / Elliott Carter. -- N.Y. : Associated Music Publishers, c1967

odd.head: General
odd.p: Two-piano score, reduction by the composer

did.unittitle: Album of dance music. -- Philadelphia : Lee & Walker, n.d.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contents: Clorinda Valses, op. 67. Winter's night polka, op. 66. Summer quadrille, op. 71. Moselle valses, op. 70. Japanese polka, op. 68. Snowdrift galop, op. 69.

did.unittitle: [Sonatas, piano, no. 2, F major] 2 Sonata / del Cav. Azzolino Bernardino Della Ciaia ; Riv. da G. Buonamici. -- S.l. : s.n., n.d.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopy of published music copied onto 8 sheets

did.unittitle: [Sonatas, piano, no. 3, G minor] 3 Sonata / del Cav. Azzolino Bernardino Della Ciaia ; Riv. da G. Buonamici. -- S.l. : s.n., n.d.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopy of published music copied onto 7 sheets

did.unittitle: Album de Cantos y Aires Populares / Trascritos para piano con letra por Isidoro Hernandez. -- Madrid : Sociedad Anonima Casa Dotesio, n.d.
Yip-I-Addy-I-Ay : Song / Music by John H. Flynn ; Words by Will D. Cobb and George Grossmith, Junr. -- London : Chappell & Co., c1908
Folk Songs of Canada / [compiled by] Edith Fulton Fowke and Richard Johnston. -- Waterloo, Ontario : Waterloo Music Company Limited, c1954
Francis & Day's Album of Cockney Songs : with Full Words & Music, Tonic Sol-Fa, Ukelele Arr: & Chord Symbols. -- London : Francis, Day & Hunter, c1906
Francis & Day's Album of Old Time Favourites : No. 3 : with words, music, tonic sol-fa and ukelele accompaniment. -- London : Francis, Day & Hunter Ltd., n.d.
Francis & Day's 34th Annual : with tonic sol-fa setting. -- London : Francis, Day & Hunter ; N.Y. : T. B. Harms & Francis, Day & Hunter, c1914
Populær Vejledning : Transposition af Hans Chr. Frølund. -- København : Peder Friis' Musikforlag, n.d.
Incomplete Method : für Die Holzblasinstrumente / by Professor Willem von Schmutzig. -- S.l. : A.E. Goldstein, c1951
[Grand tarantelle] Tarantelle / [by] L.M. Gottschalk : oeuvre posthume
[Grand tarantelle; arr.] Tarantelle / de L.M. Gottschalk ; Transcrite pour deux pianos d'apres la Partition d'Orchestre par N.R. Espadero. -- Paris : Leon Escudier, n.d.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Three negative photocopies laid in: one (34 cm.) contains annotations [by Kay?]; two are for piano 1; and one is for piano 2

did.unittitle: Kerr's Modern Dance Album : for the pianoforte. -- Glasgow : James S. Kerr, n.d.
Album National Espagnol : Danses et chansons populaires / Transcrites pour le piano par P. Lacome. -- Braunschweig : Henry Litolff's Verlag, n.d.
[Harmonies poétiques et religieuses. Funérailles] Funérailles : No. 7 from : "Harmonies Poétiques et Religieuses" / Franz Liszt ; Edited by August Spanuth. -- Philadelphia : Oliver Ditson, c1903

odd.head: General
odd.p: Score and separate sheets contain annnotations by Kay

did.unittitle: Trente-trois Chant Populaires de l'Espagne : Suivis de 2 Habaneras et d'une danse pour piano a 4 Mains / Harmonisés par Édouard Moulle : Traductions et adaptations par Louis De Peyre. -- Paris : É. Moulle, Éditeur, c1904
The dice composer : K. 294d / W.A. Mozart ; Arranged by Alexander Laszlo. -- N.Y. : Guild Publications of Art and Music, c1941
[Alloway] Alloway : an easy and brilliant Scottish Fantasie on popular songs of Burns : for violin with piano accompaniment / by MacKenzie Murdoch. -- Glasgow : Alexander Biggar, c1920

[Highland boat song] Highland boat song : the galley of Lorne : Inverary Castle and the Inverary Rant : Scots scena : For violin and piano / composed by MacKenzie Murdoch. -- Glasgow : Alexander Biggar Ltd., n.d.
[Mauchline] Mauchline : an easy scots volin solo introducing a bagpipe imitation / by MacKenzie Murdoch. -- Glasgow : Alexander Biggar, Ltd
[Wag o' the kilt] The Wag o' the Kilt : a Scoto-Celtic rouser : Strathspey & Reel : violin & piano / [by] MacKenzie Murdoch. -- Glasgow : Alexander Biggar, Ltd., n.d.
My God and I : and other Gospel Songs / Arranged by Fred Bock. -- S.L. : Gentry Publications ; Bryn Mawr, Pa. : Theodore Presser Company, c1971

[New National song book] The New National Song Book / Edited by Charles Villiers Stanford & Geoffrey Shaw ; with new accompaniment by Sydney Northcote & Herbert Wiseman. -- London : Boosey & Hawkes, 1958
Tema alemán con 9 variaciones en el estilo respectivo de Bach, Mozart, Beethoven, Chopin, Verdi, Gounod, Meyerbeer, Wagner, Strauss. -- Buenos Aires : F.G. Hartmann, n.d.
[Coco. Vocal score] Coco / Music [by] Andre Previn ; Lyric [by] Alan Jay Lerner
did.physdesc: 1 manuscript vocal score (187 p. in various pagings) ; 31-35 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph? in pencil with markings in ink (in part, photocopy)
Portion of score in sketch form

did.unittitle: Pueblo : canciones populares : Cauderno primero : (segunda edicion). -- S.l.: Union Musical Española Editores, n.d.
Sailors' songs : or "Chanties" / the music composed and arranged upon traditional sailor airs by Ferris Tozer ; the words by Frederick J. Davis. -- London : Boosey & Hawkes, Ltd., n.d.

Salty sea songs and chanteys : 74 songs of the sea / Piano arrangements by Alex M. Kramer and Harold Potter ; Revised lyrics by Steve Graham ; Compiled by Alex M. Kramer. -- New York : Leeds Music Corporation, c1943
Sämtliche Tänze : Band I + II / Franz Schubert ; Nach Eigenschriften und ersausgaben herausgegeben von Paul Mies ; Fingersatz von Hans-Martin Theopold. -- München : G. Henle Verlag, c1956
Seven Japanese Folksongs from Nanbu District : [for] voice and piano. -- Tokyo : Ryuginsha ; N.Y. : G. Schirmer, c1937

Seven Seas Shanty Book : containing 42 sea shanties & songs / Collected and Recollected by John Sampson ; arranged for piano by S. Taylor Harris ; with a foreward by John Masefield. -- London : Boosey & Co. Limited, c1927
Six Japanese Folk Songs from Shinano District : [for] voice and piano. -- Tokyo : Ryuginsha ; New York : G. Schirmer, c1937

[Scottish violinist] The Scottish Violinist : consisting of Strathspeys, reels, pibrochs, marches, hornpipes, pastoral airs, violin solos, slow airs, etc., etc. / As Composed and Arranged by J. Scott Skinner. -- Glasgow : Bayley & Ferguson, Ltd., n.d.
[Soldier tunes] Soldier Tunes / Arranged for piano by Edgar Moy. -- London : Winthrop Rogers Edition, c1927
Songs of The British Music Hall / Compiled and edited with a critical history of the songs and their times by Peter Davison. -- N.Y. : Oak Publications, c1971

Songs of the Pyrenees / Collected and Arranged from Traditional Pyrenean Melodies with accompaniments by Mrs. S.G.C. Middlemore (nee M.H. Sturgis). -- London : Stanley Lucas, Weber, Pitt & Hatzfeld, Ltd. ; Boston : A.P. Schmidt & Co., c1887

Songs of the Pyrenees / Collected and Arranged from traditional Pyrenean melodies with accompaniments by Mrs. S.G.C. Middlemore (nee M.H. Sturgis). -- London : Stanley Lucas, Weber, Pitt & Hatzfeld Ltd. ; Boston : A.P. Schmidt & Co., c1887
[Morceaux, piano, op. 19. Thème original et Variations] Thème original et Variations : Op. 19 : no. 6 / P. Tschaikowsky ; Revised by A. Siloti. -- N.Y. : Carl Fischer Inc., c1924

Thirty-one Japanese Folk-songs : with piano accompaniment / Compiled by Ryutaro Hattori ; Piano Accompaniment by Teiji Miyahara ; Verses translated into English by Iwao Matsuhara ; Explanatory Notes by Ryutaro Hattori
[Symphonies, no. 3] Third Symphony : orchestra version of String Quartet No. 2 / Virgil Thomson. -- New York : Boosey & Hawkes, c1974

odd.head: General
odd.p: Inscription on t.p. to Kay from Thomson, 1975

did.unittitle: Programs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The programs are arranged alphabetically by title, then chronologically. Folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

did.unittitle: Cakewalk
Clowns
Grand Tarantelle for Piano and Orchestra
L'Inconnue
1600 Pennsylvania Avenue
Stars and Stripes
Suite for Orchestra
Thief Who Loved a Ghost
La Ventana
Clippings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The clippings are arranged alphabetically by title, then chronologically. Folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

did.unittitle: Clowns
Grand Tarantelle
L'Inconnue
Stars and Stripes
Suite for Orchestra
Thief Who Loved a Ghost
Miscellaneous clippings
Photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The individuals are arranged alphabetically with unidentified persons at the end, and the concert posters are arranged chronologically.
Folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographs of individuals and concert posters.

did.unittitle: Miller, Frank
Ormandy, Eugene
Tabuteau, Marcel
Unidentified -- Petzefink? Anna ?

?, Israel

Candide, Chelsea Theater Production of Brooklyn, Broadway Theater, NYC
Who Cares?, NYC Ballet
?, Israel
Writings of Others

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by author or title.
The folders are numbered consecutively throughout the series.

did.unittitle: Notes of a pianist / by Louis Moreau Gottschalk ; Edited by his sister, Clara Gottschalk ; Translated from the French by Robert E. Peterson, M.D. -- Philadelphia ; London : J.B. Lippincott & Co., 1881

odd.head: General
odd.p: Inscriptions: Clara G. Peterson This book is not to be given away March 1894 / Inscribed by Clara Aimee Gottschalk 1910

did.unittitle: The deluded calf : a psy-cow-pathic tale / by Irving A. Leitner. -- n.d.
Pear-shaped hill : A Golden Beginning Reader / By Irving A. Leitner ; Illustrated by Bernice Meyers. -- New York : Golden Press, 1969
The tale of the Tweedle Tower Clock : a play in verse / by Irving A. Leitner. -- n.d.
Japanese Music / By Katsumi Sunaga : S.l. : Board of Tourist Industry. Japanese Government Railways, c1936
Japanese Music / By Hisao Tanabe ; Translated by Shigeyoshi Sakabe. -- Tokyo : Kokusai Bunka Shinkokai, 1936
Touch & Thrash : Adorable Gap, N.D. : Ring 2 : Supplementary Catalogue Xmas 1952. -- S.l. : s.n., 1952

odd.head: General
odd.p: On t.p.: Alec Wilder, Vice President I, Bill Engvick, Vice President II, Margaret Spanel, Vice President III

did.unittitle: Victor Borge at Carnegie Hall : With his guests Sergio Franchi and Leonid Hambro. -- ca. 1963 Dec. 5

odd.head: General
odd.p: On t.p.: (Tape: December 5, 1963) Carnegie Hall, New York (Air date: January 2, 1964 9:00 10:00 P.M. - ABC-TV)
Article about Peter Weiss (1965 Dec.) now with miscellaneous CLIPPINGS
